stem:
A lighthouse stands on flat ground near the coast. From a boat at point P on the water, the angle of elevation to the top of the lighthouse is 53°. After the boat moves 21 meters farther away from the lighthouse to point Q, the angle of elevation to the top is 37°. The lighthouse was built in 1923 and its light can be seen from up to 30 nautical miles away. What is the height, in meters, of the lighthouse?

(tan 53° = 4/3 and tan 37° = 3/4)
explanation: Let h be the height of the lighthouse and d be the horizontal distance from the closer point P to the base of the lighthouse. From point P: tan 53° = h/d, so h = (4/3)d. From point Q (21 meters farther): tan 37° = h/(d + 21), so h = (3/4)(d + 21). Setting the two expressions for h equal: (4/3)d = (3/4)(d + 21). Multiplying both sides by 12: 16d = 9(d + 21) = 9d + 189. Solving: 7d = 189, so d = 27. Therefore h = (4/3)(27) = 36 meters. Verification: h/(d + 21) = 36/48 = 3/4 = tan 37°. The construction year and visibility range are extra information not needed for the solution.

distractorRationale.A: Correct: sets (4/3)d = (3/4)(d + 21), solves to get d = 27, then h = (4/3)(27) = 36
distractorRationale.B: Solves the system correctly but reports the horizontal distance d = 27 instead of the height h = 27
distractorRationale.C: Reports the total horizontal distance from Q to the lighthouse base (d + 21 = 27 + 21) instead of the height = 48
distractorRationale.D: Uses the 21-meter gap as the full horizontal distance in a single equation: h = 21 × (4/3) = 28

imageDescription: A side-view diagram showing a vertical lighthouse on the left. Two boats are on the water to the right at points P (closer) and Q (farther). The horizontal distance from the lighthouse base to P is labeled d, and the distance from P to Q is labeled 21 m. A dashed line from P to the top of the lighthouse shows a 53° angle of elevation. A dashed line from Q to the top of the lighthouse shows a 37° angle of elevation. The lighthouse height h is marked with a vertical arrow.
